info.title: DorkOS API
info.description: REST/SSE API for Claude Code sessions, built with the Claude Agent SDK.

approvalTitle.description: Full permission prompt sentence from SDK

approvalDisplayName.description: Short noun phrase for the tool action

approvalBlockedPath.description: File path that triggered the permission

approvalDecisionReason.description: Why this permission request was triggered

UiSidebarTab.description: Sidebar tab id, e.g. a built-in ('overview', 'sessions', 'schedules', 'connections'). The sidebar tab strip exists only in the embedded (Obsidian) app; on the web cockpit there is no strip, so switching a sidebar tab is a no-op there.

sessionId.description: Canonical session id; differs from the request id for a new session

isDefault.description: Whether this is the default model

supportsEffort.description: Whether this model supports effort levels

supportedEffortLevels.description: Available effort levels for this model

supportsFastMode.description: Whether this model supports fast mode

supportsAutoMode.description: Whether this model supports auto mode

supportsAdaptiveThinking.description: Claude decides when and how much to think

InboxStatusFilter.description: Filter messages by status. Defaults to "pending" (deliverable, unread messages) so budget-rejected failures never surface silently next to real deliverables. Pass "failed" to see budget-rejected/dead-lettered messages, "delivered" for already-read ones (metadata only — the payload is removed once a message completes), or "all" for every status.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name. Falls back to name (slug) when absent.

persona.description: System prompt persona text injected into Claude sessions
persona.example: You are backend-bot, an expert in REST API design...

personaEnabled.description: Whether persona text is injected into system prompt

color.description: CSS color override for visual identity (e.g., "#6366f1")

isSystem.description: System agents are auto-managed and cannot be deleted

EnabledToolGroups.description: Per-domain tool group enable/disable. undefined = inherit global default. Binding tools follow adapter toggle. Trace tools follow relay toggle.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name. Falls back to name (slug) when absent.

persona.description: System prompt persona text injected into Claude sessions
persona.example: You are backend-bot, an expert in REST API design...

personaEnabled.description: Whether persona text is injected into system prompt

color.description: CSS color override for visual identity (e.g., "#6366f1")

isSystem.description: System agents are auto-managed and cannot be deleted

displayName.description: Human-readable display name. Falls back to name (slug) when absent.

persona.description: System prompt persona text injected into Claude sessions
persona.example: You are backend-bot, an expert in REST API design...

personaEnabled.description: Whether persona text is injected into system prompt

sidebarTab.description: Sidebar tab id, e.g. a built-in ('overview', 'sessions', 'schedules', 'connections'). The sidebar tab strip exists only in the embedded (Obsidian) app; on the web cockpit switching a sidebar tab is a no-op.

captureCurrent.description: Snapshot the live arrangement (enabled extensions + chrome) when forking the active Shape.

get.description: Aggregates sessions across every registered runtime (ADR-0310). Runtimes that fail or time out degrade to `warnings[]` entries with partial results.

200.description: Session list envelope (merged across runtimes, sorted by updatedAt desc)

get.summary: List recent sessions across all agents
get.description: Fans out session listing across every registered agent (DOR-329), merges by `updatedAt` descending, trims to `limit`, and returns a per-agent latest-activity map plus per-runtime `warnings[]` (ADR-0310).

200.description: Recent sessions envelope with per-agent activity map

post.description: TRIGGERS a turn and returns immediately — it does NOT stream tokens (ADR-0264). The turn runs server-side and its events are delivered solely on the durable `GET /api/sessions/{id}/events` stream (the single delivery path). The `202` body carries the CANONICAL session id: for a brand-new session this is the real id assigned during the turn (it differs from the client-supplied id), so the client re-keys its URL and `/events` subscription to it. To avoid missing the turn, a client should be subscribed to `/events` before (or concurrently with) this POST.

202.description: Turn accepted and started; body carries the canonical session id

get.description: Always-on Server-Sent Events stream — the single delivery path for session state (spec chat-stream-reconnection, ADR-0264/ADR-0266). NO feature flag or `enableCrossClientSync` gate. On a COLD connect it emits one `snapshot` event (a SessionSnapshot: completed messages, in-progress turn, status, non-expired pending interactions, and the resume `cursor`) then goes live, emitting one SessionEvent per frame. Each LIVE frame is preceded by an `id: <sessionId>-<epoch>-<seq>` line; the browser echoes it back as `Last-Event-ID` on reconnect. On a RESUME connect — `Last-Event-ID: <sessionId>-<epoch>-<seq>` header OR `?after=<cursor>` query — it SKIPS the snapshot and replays only events with `seq` greater than the cursor, then goes live. A cursor the server cannot serve gap-free (mismatched epoch after a restart, or one trimmed past the replay buffer) falls back to the cold snapshot path instead of resuming. A `: keepalive` comment is sent every ~15s and `X-Accel-Buffering: no` defeats proxy buffering. Collapses DOR-73 Path A (pull) + Path B (re-emit) into one snapshot+replay mechanism; the single always-on delivery path.

schema.description: Resume cursor; replay events with seq greater than this.

parameters.description: Resume cursor; replay events with seq greater than this.
parameters.name: after

schema.description: Resume token `<sessionId>-<epoch>-<seq>`; replays only the gap. A token from a previous server process (epoch mismatch) or beyond the replay buffer falls back to a cold snapshot.

parameters.description: Resume token `<sessionId>-<epoch>-<seq>`; replays only the gap. A token from a previous server process (epoch mismatch) or beyond the replay buffer falls back to a cold snapshot.
parameters.name: Last-Event-ID

200.description: SSE stream. Cold connect: a `snapshot` event then `id:`-framed SessionEvents. Resume connect: replayed-then-live `id:`-framed SessionEvents (no snapshot).

schema.description: A SessionSnapshot (cold connect) followed by SessionEvent frames.

get.summary: Get task state from session transcript

post.description: TRIGGERS a RUNTIME-fulfilled command intent (currently `compact`) and returns immediately (DOR-109, ADR-0273/ADR-0264). The runtime expands the neutral intent into its native mechanism (Claude: bare `/compact`; OpenCode: `session.summarize`), and the outcome — a compaction — is delivered solely on the durable `GET /api/sessions/{id}/events` stream (e.g. a `compact_boundary`), NOT in this response. The client-native intents (`clear`, `context`) are handled entirely client-side and never reach this route. Capability-gated: a runtime that does not support the intent (e.g. Codex) returns `422` and the adapter is never called — never a silent no-op. Mirrors the message trigger: `409` SESSION_LOCKED when a turn is already running.

requestBody.description: Optional trailing instructions the user typed after the intent token (e.g. `/compact focus on the API changes`). Forwarded to runtimes whose native mechanism accepts guidance (claude-code); ignored by those whose mechanism takes none (opencode).

202.description: Intent accepted and started; body carries the session id

400.description: Invalid session id or malformed request body

422.description: Unknown intent, or the session runtime does not support it

post.summary: Dispatch a generative-UI widget agent action
post.description: A click on an `agent`-kind widget action. Injects a structured `<ui_action>` block as the next user turn (trigger-only, 202; the turn streams over /events). Mirrors the message trigger: 409 SESSION_LOCKED when a turn is already running.

202.description: Action accepted; the turn is delivered over /events

post.summary: Read a ui:// MCP App resource
post.description: Reads a `ui://` MCP App resource (SEP-1865) for client rendering. The server opens its own short-lived MCP client using connection config it captured internally — the stdio/http config never travels to the client. Enforces the `ui://` scheme, `text/html` mime, and that the server belongs to the session's MCP set.

200.description: The resource body plus its sandbox metadata

404.description: Session, server, or captured config not found

get.description: Returns models available to the user. Serves SDK-reported models if cached, otherwise returns defaults.
Returns capabilities for all registered runtimes, keyed by type string, along with the default runtime type.

supportsPlugins.description: Whether this runtime can load plugins.

description.description: Optional helper copy shown beneath the label in rich pickers.

permissionModes.description: Structured permission-mode capability. `supported: false, values: []` means no picker is shown.

get.description: Browse directories on the server filesystem. Restricted to the home directory for security.
Returns the server's boundary-resolved default working directory.

404.description: Run not found or not active

200.description: Message details with per-endpoint delivery breakdown

get.description: Defaults to `pending` messages only, so budget-rejected `failed` messages never surface next to deliverables unless a caller explicitly opts in via `?status=`.

parameters.description: Filter messages by status. Defaults to "pending" (deliverable, unread messages) so budget-rejected failures never surface silently next to real deliverables. Pass "failed" to see budget-rejected/dead-lettered messages, "delivered" for already-read ones (metadata only — the payload is removed once a message completes), or "all" for every status.
parameters.name: status

payload.description: Envelope payload read from Maildir; null once the message is acknowledged

get.summary: SSE event stream for relay activity
get.description: Server-Sent Events stream for real-time relay activity. Supports server-side subject filtering via query param. Event types: relay_connected, relay_message, relay_delivery, relay_dead_letter, relay_metrics.

post.summary: Force refetch of a source marketplace.json

get.description: Without projectPath: one entry per installation across all scopes (global roots plus every registered agent's local installs), each tagged with scope and agent identity. With projectPath: the merged view for that single project — one entry per package name.

get.summary: List every installation of a package
get.description: Every installation of the named package across all scopes (global + each agent), each enriched with capability counts (commands, skills, hooks).

post.summary: Garbage-collect cached packages, keeping the N most recent per name

get.summary: List installable packages (aggregated from every enabled source)
Get package details (fetches, validates, builds a preview)

200.description: Package manifest, staged path, permission preview, and optional README

post.summary: Build a permission preview without installing

200.description: Permission preview, manifest, and staged path
Install result from the type-specific flow

post.summary: Advisory update check (pass apply:true to actually update)

200.description: Update advisory result (and any applied reinstalls)

post.summary: Begin the device flow to link this instance to a DorkOS account
post.description: Requests a device code from the DorkOS cloud and starts a background poll. Returns the user code + verification URI for the human to approve; poll GET /api/cloud/link/status for the outcome. Independent of local login (config.auth.enabled).

userCode.description: The 8-character code the human enters at the cloud.

verificationUri.description: Where the human goes to approve the link.

expiresAt.description: ISO timestamp after which the code is dead.

502.description: Could not reach the DorkOS cloud

post.summary: Unlink this instance (best-effort cloud revoke, then clear local state)

200.description: Linked state, account label, and last heartbeat

get.description: Returns every installed Shape with its display name, active flag (`ui.shapes.active`), and fork lineage.

post.description: Enables the Shape’s extensions, resolves connections, stands up schedules, offers agents (never forces one), and records the active Shape. Only "Shape not installed" is fatal (404); every other missing piece degrades to a `warnings[]` entry. `applied.layout` carries the chrome the client restores without a second fetch (spec §5/§9).

200.description: Apply result (chrome + activated extensions + created schedules, warnings, offers)

post.description: Clones an installed Shape into a new, independently-editable one and stamps `lineage`. `captureCurrent` snapshots the live arrangement when forking the active Shape.

409.description: Fork name is invalid or already taken

get.description: Aggregates `listToolkits()` across every registered connector provider, deduped by slug, degrading one unreachable provider to a `warnings[]` entry (ADR-0310).

200.description: Connectable services plus per-provider degradation warnings

get.summary: Recommend how to connect a service (relay-adapter > gateway > raw-mcp)
get.description: Returns ranked recommendations for a service, best first: a purpose-built relay adapter (rank 0) outranks a gateway backend (rank 1), which outranks a raw-MCP baseline (rank 2). This is the routing surface the "Connect to Slack" and "Connect to my Gmail" evals assert against.

200.description: Ranked connector recommendations (ascending by rank) plus degradation warnings

post.summary: Begin a connect flow for a toolkit
post.description: Starts an OAuth/connect flow on the named provider and returns a consent URL plus a pollable flow id. Secrets stay server-side — the response is reference-shaped.

200.description: Connect started; carries the authorize URL and a pollable flow id

400.description: Validation error, unknown toolkit, or a duplicate single-account connect

get.summary: Poll a connect flow to completion
get.description: Polls an in-flight connect flow. Failure is typed on the result (`status: "failed"`), never thrown. On `connected`, the new account is bound to its owning provider for later routing.

200.description: The pollable connect state (pending | connected | failed)

get.description: Aggregates connected accounts across providers with per-provider degradation. The server-only `provider` field is stripped and no connection details ever reach the client (spec §Security).

200.description: Connected accounts (provider stripped) plus degradation warnings

delete.description: Revokes the account at its owning provider and clears its routing binding. Idempotent — disconnecting an unknown or already-removed id still resolves 204.

get.summary: A session's connector surface (attached accounts + warnings)
get.description: Lists the connected accounts explicitly attached to a session, each with its exposure state, plus per-account warnings for attached accounts that cannot be exposed right now (the `toolServerForAccount` null branch: expired / revoked / unavailable).

200.description: Attached accounts and per-account degradation warnings

post.summary: Attach a connected account to a session (the consent point)
post.description: Attaches an account to a session so its tools are exposed as an MCP tool server, and re-shows the custody disclosure (spec §Detailed Design 3). A known account whose connection resolves null still attaches (consent recorded) but is reported unexposed via a warning; no connection detail ever crosses to the client.

200.description: The account attached; carries its status, the custody disclosure, and any warning

delete.summary: Detach a connected account from a session (idempotent)
delete.description: Removes the consent binding so the account is no longer exposed to the session. Idempotent — detaching an unattached account still resolves 204.
